title: Long-Horizon Manipulation via Trace-Conditioned VLA Planning
abstract: Existing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models demonstrate impressive capabilities in single-step or short-horizon manipulation tasks but struggle with long-horizon tasks requiring multi-step planning. We propose Trace-Conditioned VLA Planning, a hierarchical framework where a high-level VLM first generates a complete manipulation trace as a sequence of 2D keypoints representing the expected end-effector trajectory, and a low-level VLA policy then executes actions conditioned on this trace. This 'plan-then-execute' design enables the VLA to maintain awareness of the global task structure while generating locally optimal actions. Our approach significantly improves success rates on 10+ step tabletop manipulation tasks compared to end-to-end VLA baselines.
methodology: The framework consists of two stages: (1) Trace Generation - A VLM (e.g., GPT-4V or Gemini) takes the current visual observation and natural language instruction as input, and outputs a sequence of 2D keypoints representing the planned end-effector trajectory. This trace captures the global structure of the manipulation plan. (2) Trace-Conditioned Execution - A VLA policy receives the visual observation, language instruction, and the generated trace as inputs, and produces robot actions at each timestep. The trace provides a 'roadmap' that guides the policy through multi-step sequences. During execution, the trace can be re-planned if the VLM detects significant deviation from the expected trajectory.
